examples.title: Packet Forwarding Algorithm (Store-and-Forward)
examples.description: Demonstrates how a router processes and forwards packets using store-and-forward technique
examples.code:
PROCEDURE RouterProcessPacket(packet)
BEGIN
 // Step 1: Receive entire packet
 inputBuffer.receive(packet)
 
 // Step 2: Processing delay - examine header
 header = packet.extractHeader()
 destAddress = header.destinationIP
 
 // Step 3: Error checking (FCS/CRC)
 IF NOT packet.verifyChecksum() THEN
 packet.drop()
 RETURN error
 END IF
 
 // Step 4: Lookup routing table
 nextHop = routingTable.lookup(destAddress)
 
 // Step 5: Queuing delay - wait for output port
 outputQueue.enqueue(packet)
 
 // Step 6: Transmission delay - send bits
 WHILE NOT packet.transmitted() DO
 outputLink.send(packet.bits)
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN success
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the step-by-step process a router follows: receiving the packet, checking for errors (processing delay), determining the next hop through routing table lookup, queuing for output, and transmitting the packet bits onto the link (transmission delay).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is packet size

examples.title: Packet Fragmentation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for fragmenting large packets when MTU is exceeded
examples.code:
PROCEDURE FragmentPacket(packet, mtu)
BEGIN
 header = packet.header
 payload = packet.payload
 payloadSize = payload.length
 
 // Calculate max payload per fragment
 maxPayloadPerFragment = mtu - headerSize
 
 // Calculate number of fragments needed
 numFragments = CEIL(payloadSize / maxPayloadPerFragment)
 
 fragments = []
 offset = 0
 
 FOR i = 1 TO numFragments DO
 fragment = NEW Packet
 
 // Set header fields for fragmentation
 fragment.header.sourceIP = header.sourceIP
 fragment.header.destIP = header.destIP
 fragment.header.fragmentOffset = offset
 fragment.header.moreFragmentsFlag = (i < numFragments)
 fragment.header.identification = header.identification
 
 // Copy payload portion
 fragment.payload = payload.substring(offset, maxPayloadPerFragment)
 
 fragments.append(fragment)
 offset = offset + maxPayloadPerFragment
 END FOR
 
 RETURN fragments
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm breaks down a large packet into smaller fragments when the MTU of the outgoing interface is smaller than the packet size. Each fragment carries offset information to allow proper reassembly at the destination.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is payload size

examples.title: End-to-End Packet Transmission (Simple)
examples.description: Demonstrates packet transmission from source to destination through multiple routers
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SendPacket(source, destination, data)
BEGIN
 // Create packet with header
 packet = NEW Packet
 packet.header.sourceIP = source.address
 packet.header.destIP = destination.address
 packet.payload = data
 packet.header.sequenceNumber = nextSequenceNumber
 
 // Transmission begins
 currentRouter = source.connectedRouter
 
 WHILE currentRouter != destination DO
 // Store-and-forward at each router
 packet = RouterProcessPacket(packet)
 
 // Routing decision
 nextRouter = packet.routeTo(destination)
 currentRouter = nextRouter
 END WHILE
 
 // Deliver to destination
 destination.receive(packet)
 
 RETURN success
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the end-to-end packet transmission process. The packet goes through multiple routers, each performing store-and-forward processing, until it reaches the destination host.
examples.timeComplexity: O(h × n) where h = hops, n = packet size

examples.title: Simple Priority Queuing (QoS)
examples.description: Demonstrates a QoS mechanism with priority queues for different traffic types
examples.code:
PROCEDURE PriorityScheduler(queues)
BEGIN
 // queues[0] = high priority (VoIP)
 // queues[1] = medium priority (video)
 // queues[2] = low priority (best-effort)
 
 packet = NULL
 
 // Check highest priority queue first
 FOR priority = 0 TO 2 DO
 IF NOT queues[priority].isEmpty() THEN
 packet = queues[priority].dequeue()
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF packet != NULL THEN
 outputLink.transmit(packet)
 ELSE
 // No packets to send - idle
 WAIT for packet arrival
 END IF
 
 RETURN
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This shows a simple priority queuing QoS mechanism where packets are dequeued based on priority. Higher priority traffic (like VoIP) is served first, ensuring low-latency delivery for time-sensitive applications.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(w) where w is queue depth

examples.title: Calculate Total End-to-End Delay
examples.description: Algorithm to compute total delay a packet experiences in packet-switched network
examples.explanation: This function calculates the total end-to-end delay by summing all four delay components at each hop in the network path. It's used for network performance analysis and planning.
examples.timeComplexity: O(h) where h is number of hops
